title: Boat Rental in Zanzibar: Tips, Costs and Best Boats for 2026
html:
For exploring Zanzibar's turquoise waters by sea, renting a boat from Stone Town or Kendwa Beach offers freedom and access to hidden coves. A half-day motorboat charter typically costs from TZS 800,000 to TZS 1,500,000, and you can find verified options on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace for boat rentals.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Zanzibar?

Rental costs vary widely by boat type and duration, starting from about TZS 400,000 for a shared tour and reaching TZS 5,000,000 for a crewed catamaran full day. For a private half-day on a small motorboat, budget TZS 800,000 to TZS 1,500,000. A full-day catamaran charter for up to ten guests runs between TZS 2,500,000 and TZS 4,500,000. Crewed luxury yachts with skipper and crew often exceed TZS 5,000,000, especially during high season. You can compare verified boat listings and prices on yacht charter Zanzibar pages to find the best fit.

Where to set off in Zanzibar

The main departure points are Stone Town's port, Fumba Beach, Kendwa Beach, and Nungwi Harbour. Stone Town provides easy access to Prison Island and the sandbanks of the Menai Bay Conservation Area. Fumba, on the southwest coast, is a quieter launch point for trips to Kwale Island and the Jozani Forest mangroves. Kendwa and Nungwi, on the northern tip, are ideal for exploring Mnemba Atoll and its marine reserve. Other popular spots include Kizimkazi for dolphin-watching excursions and Matemwe, near the eastern reef.

Which boat to rent

The right boat depends on your group size and itinerary, from fast dinghies to spacious catamarans.

  1. Small motorboat (10, 20 ft), Best for up to 6 guests, ideal for short trips to nearby islands; from TZS 700,000 half-day.
  2. Sports cruiser (Azimut or Axopar models), For 6, 8 guests, faster and more comfortable for longer distances; from TZS 1,500,000 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon or similar), Spacious for 8, 12 guests, stable and good for day sails with snorkelling stops; from TZS 2,500,000 full-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (Fairline, Sunseeker or Maiora), Crewed for up to 10 guests, includes expert skipper and on-board amenities; from TZS 4,000,000 full-day.
  5. Traditional dhow, Authentic wooden sailing boat for up to 12 guests, perfect for sunset cruises; from TZS 600,000 for a 2-hour trip.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Zanzibar?

No licence is required if you charter a boat with a local skipper, which is the standard arrangement for rental in Zanzibar. All boats listed on BoatHire24 include a qualified skipper and crew as standard, ensuring safety and compliance with local regulations. If you wish to self-drive a very small vessel (such as a dinghy), the operator can confirm any specific requirements, but bareboat charters are rare. The operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking.

For the best experience, book a crewed boat and let the local experts navigate the changing currents around Unguja. A genuine tip:

Even in calm weather, the channel between Zanzibar and the mainland can kick up quickly, a local skipper who knows the sandbanks is worth the extra cost, especially in the afternoon sea breeze.

When is the best season for boat rental in Zanzibar?

The best season runs from June to October during the southeast monsoon (Kaskazi wind), when seas are calm and visibility is excellent. The northeast monsoon (Kusi) from December to March also offers good conditions but with slightly stronger winds. The rainy seasons (April, May and November) often bring rougher seas and reduced visibility, making boat trips less reliable. For 2026, aim for the peak months of July, August and September to enjoy the clearest waters around Mnemba Atoll.

At a glance

  • Price range: TZS 400,000 to TZS 5,000,000 per trip
  • Peak months: July, August, September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Max passenger capacity: up to 12 guests on most catamarans
  • Currency: Tanzanian Shilling (TZS)
  • Licence needed: No (skipper provided)

Questions about boat rental in Zanzibar

Can I rent a boat just for a sunset cruise?

Sunset cruises are very popular, typically lasting about two hours from Kendwa or Stone Town, and cost from TZS 500,000 for a private dhow.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Zanzibar?

Booking at least two weeks ahead is recommended during peak season (July to September) to secure your preferred vessel and departure time.

Are meals and drinks included in the charter price?

Many full-day charters include a barbecue lunch and soft drinks; check the listing details or confirm with the operator before booking.

What happens if the weather is bad on my charter day?

Most operators offer a free reschedule or full refund if the trip is cancelled due to unsafe conditions; this is stated in the terms on each boat listing.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Zanzibar or list your own. To book a vessel from Stone Town or Kendwa, browse the Salty Sun Charters listing for a fully crewed motor yacht. If you own a boat, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach travellers planning their 2026 escape to Zanzibar.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Zanzibar and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
